metadata.summary: Advanced TikTok tactics: hook mastery, algorithm optimization, posting cadence, trends, conversion funnels, growth loops. Watch-time optimization outperforms frequency.

content.title: TikTok Growth Strategies
content.summary: Advanced TikTok tactics for accounts past the setup phase: hook formula mastery, algorithm signal optimization, posting cadence, trend usage, conversion funnel, and growth loops (duets, stitches, series cliffhangers). The core rule: watch-time optimization outperforms posting frequency — 3 videos at 70%+ completion rate outperform 7 videos at 40% completion in algorithm distribution.

why.p: The TikTok For You Page algorithm weights watch time and completion rate above all other signals. Accounts that optimize hooks and structure before scaling frequency grow faster with less effort. Hook formulas (curiosity, challenge, promise, relatability, controversy) produce measurably different results per niche; testing 5 variants before scaling is the minimum valid sample size.

list.item: Generating hook variants (5+ per video) in different formula types for A/B testing
Analyzing watch-time drop-off patterns and recommending structural changes
Drafting trend adaptation scripts given a specific sound/format and niche context
Creating duet/stitch strategy briefs for competitor or niche creator engagement
Generating cliffhanger series arcs ("Part 1"/"Part 2") for series content strategy
Real-time trend identification — agents cannot browse the TikTok Discover or For You Page
Selecting which trending sounds to use — sound trend data expires in 24-48 hours
Predicting which specific video will go viral
Running TikTok Ads campaigns — paid media task with separate workflow
Any automated posting — violates ToS and risks account ban

section.name: hook-formulas-and-algorithm
section.p: Five hook formula types, visual hook rules, algorithm signal weights, and posting cadence targets.

rule.statement: Test at least 5 hook variants (one per formula type) per topic before scaling frequency; below 5 variants is an insufficient sample size.
rule.rationale: Hook formula effectiveness is niche-dependent; what works for coding content fails for B2B SaaS. Only performance data from real publishing reveals which formula resonates.

list.item: Curiosity: "Here's why [unexpected thing]..." (e.g., "Here's why your startup will fail...")
Challenge: "Stop doing [common thing]" (e.g., "Stop using Google Docs for specs")
Promise: "How I [achieved result]" (e.g., "How I got 10K users with $0 ads")
Relatability: "POV: You're a [role] and..." (e.g., "POV: You're a founder and...")
Controversy: "Unpopular opinion: [take]" (e.g., "Unpopular opinion: MVP is dead")

rule.statement: Start mid-action, not with an intro; the first frame must contain movement, face close to camera, or unexpected visual.
rule.rationale: TikTok's algorithm measures average watch time in the first 3 seconds separately from total watch time; a static intro frame causes instant scroll-past.

list.item: Text on screen must appear in the first frame (not after intro)
Face should be close to camera, not far away
Movement in the first frame: hand gesture, item held up, screen recording starting

rule.statement: Optimize watch time and completion rate before optimizing for likes; completion rate carries more algorithmic weight than any engagement metric.
rule.rationale: The For You Page distribution algorithm weights watch time and completion rate "very high" and likes "medium." Accounts that optimize completion outgrow accounts that optimize for likes at 3-5x speed.

list.item: Watch time: very high weight — optimize with strong hooks and fast pacing
Completion rate: very high weight — keep initial videos short to hit 90%+ completion
Shares: high weight — make content share-worthy by being useful or entertaining
Comments: high weight — ask questions; controversial takes drive comment volume
Saves: high weight — actionable content (checklists, tips) drives saves
Likes: medium weight — secondary metric, do not optimize at expense of completion

rule.statement: Post 3 videos at 70%+ completion rate instead of 7 videos at 40% completion; watch-time optimization outperforms posting frequency.
rule.rationale: Low completion rate signals poor content quality; the algorithm stops distributing accounts with chronically low completion, regardless of volume.

list.item: Minimum: 1 post/day, every day — never skip in first 90 days
Ideal: 3-5 posts/day once content bank is established
Best times: 7-9am (morning scroll), 12-2pm (work breaks), 7-10pm (peak usage) — test for your specific audience

section.name: growth-loops-and-trends
section.p: Trend adaptation workflow, TikTok conversion funnel, growth loops (duets/stitches/series), real case studies.

rule.statement: Post trend-adapted content within 24-48 hours of a trend starting; trends that are 72+ hours old deliver 50-80% fewer views.
rule.rationale: TikTok's algorithm boosts trending sound usage during the early adoption phase; late adoption gets no boost and may look derivative.

list.item: Check Discover page daily; browse For You Page for 10 min
Identify the trend pattern: structure, twist, and audio type
Adapt to your niche: same structure, your industry context, your unique angle
Film and post within 24 hours of identifying the trend

section.name: conversion-funnel
section.p: Views → Profile visits → Link clicks → Leads/Sales. The secondary path: Followers → Repeat exposure → Trust → Conversion.

rule.statement: Optimize the link in bio for mobile conversion; the profile visit → link click step is the highest drop-off point in the TikTok funnel.
rule.rationale: Most TikTok traffic lands on a profile page then clicks bio link; a non-mobile-optimized landing page wastes the entire top-of-funnel effort.

list.item: Link in bio: Linktree or direct landing page (mobile-optimized)
Pinned video: place your best converting video at the top of profile
Comment replies with CTA: respond to questions with link mention
Lead capture: free resource (checklist, template), Discord invite, newsletter

rule.statement: Target duet/stitch creators with 50K-500K followers in your niche; mega-creators (1M+) deliver lower follow-back rates and less targeted audiences.
rule.rationale: Mid-tier niche creators have more engaged, specific audiences that match B2B/SaaS ICPs. Mega-creator audiences are too broad for most product offers.

list.item: Duets/Stitches: leverage others' audiences, expect 2-5x views on duet videos
Series content ("Part 1", "Part 2"): keep viewers coming back, higher follower conversion
Cliffhangers: "Part 2 drops tomorrow" increases profile visit rate 3-5x — only use if Part 2 is ready to publish within 24 hours
Collaborations: tag other creators in your niche for shared audience access

example.title: Developer tool — 0 to 150K followers in 6 months
example.code: Strategy: Daily coding tips (15-30 sec), trending sounds + code tutorials, "Day X of learning [language]" series Results: 150K followers, 2M+ total views, 5K newsletter signups, $10K in course sales

example.title: B2B service — 200 to 25K followers in 4 months
example.code: Strategy: Industry myth-busting, quick tips for target ICP, case study storytimes, duets with industry content Results: 25K followers, 15 qualified leads/month, 3 enterprise clients ($50K+ deals)

section.name: analytics-review-workflow

rule.statement: Review analytics weekly; focus on watch-time graphs (where do viewers drop off?) before any other metric.
rule.rationale: Drop-off point analysis reveals structural problems (weak transition, too-long intro) that can be fixed in the next video; other metrics only confirm results, not causes.

section.reference: Script to process TikTok Analytics CSV and surface watch-time patterns

list.item: Run at least 5 hook variants per topic before concluding which formula works for your niche — sample size below 5 is statistically meaningless
Cliffhanger series ("Part 2 drops tomorrow") increase profile visit rate by 3-5x but only work if Part 2 publishes within 24 hours — do not start a series without the full script ready
Duet/stitch content targeting creators with 50K-500K followers in your niche delivers better follow-back rate than targeting mega-creators
Post your highest-effort content during your audience's peak activity window (check Analytics → Followers → Activity); the first 30 minutes of engagement velocity determines FYP distribution
Watch-time optimization is more important than posting frequency; 3 videos at 70%+ completion outperform 7 videos at 40% completion
Build a content bank of 10-14 videos before launching a new series format; consistency during algorithm testing phase is critical

antipattern.description: Agents generate hooks that are grammatically correct but tonally flat — TikTok hooks need urgency and pattern interruption, which requires explicit prompting ("write this hook as if the viewer is about to scroll away").
antipattern.reason: Default tone is neutral; TikTok's medium demands attention-grabbing urgency.

antipattern.description: Series content drafted by agents often lacks a genuine cliffhanger — the "reveal" in Part 2 must be compelling enough to justify a follow; validate manually before posting Part 1.
antipattern.reason: Agents tend to generate logical conclusions rather than psychological hooks.

antipattern.description: Watch-time analysis requires actual data; agents asked to "predict" drop-off points without data will hallucinate statistics.
antipattern.reason: Only real analytics reveal drop-off patterns; predictions without data are fabrications.

antipattern.description: Trend adaptation scripts sound generic unless the trend's exact sound, text overlay style, and visual pattern are described in detail to the agent.
antipattern.reason: Agents need concrete reference material, not abstract trend names.

antipattern.description: Do not ask an agent to select which hashtags to use — TikTok hashtag strategy is real-time and requires Creative Center access; use 3-5 niche hashtags chosen by a human.
antipattern.reason: Real-time trend data is outside agent training scope.

template.purpose: Prompt pattern to generate 5 hook variants per formula type as JSON
Script to process TikTok Analytics CSV and surface watch-time patterns
